CGAL::Rotational_sweep_visibility_2.name: CGAL::Rotational_sweep_visibility_2
CGAL::Rotational_sweep_visibility_2.brief: This class is a model of the concept Visibility_2 can answer visibility queries within a polygon that may have holes.
CGAL::Rotational_sweep_visibility_2.detailed: The algorithm does not require preprocessing. It relies on the algorithm of T. Asano [1] based on angular plane sweep, with a time complexity of $O(n \log n)$ in the number of vertices. Arrangement_2_ is the type used to represent the input environment. It must be an instance of CGAL::Arrangement_2 , where its CGAL::Arrangement_2::Traits_2 must be an instance of CGAL::Arr_segment_traits_2 , or of CGAL::Arr_non_caching_segment_traits_2 . RegularizationCategory indicates whether the output should be regularized. It can be specified by one of the following: #Tag_true or #Tag_false , where #Tag_false is the default value. Is model of Visibility_2 CGAL::Simple_polygon_visibility_2 CGAL::Triangular_expansion_visibility_2

members.brief: identifies whether the regularized visibility area is computed (either #Tag_true or #Tag_false ).

members.name: attach
members.brief: Attaches the given arrangement to the visibility object.
members.detailed: This operation takes $O(1)$ as the class does no preprocessing. In case the object is already attached to another arrangement, the visibility object gets detached before being attached to arr .

CGAL::Simple_polygon_visibility_2.name: CGAL::Simple_polygon_visibility_2
CGAL::Simple_polygon_visibility_2.brief: This class is a model of the concept Visibility_2 can answer visibility queries within a simple polygon with no holes.
CGAL::Simple_polygon_visibility_2.detailed: This class implements the algorithm of B.Joe and R.B.Simpson [4] . The algorithm is a modification and extension of the linear time algorithm of Lee [5] . It computes the visibility region from a viewpoint that is in the interior or on the boundary of the polygon. While scanning the boundary the algorithm uses a stack to manipulate the vertices, and ultimately yields the visibility region. For each scanned edge, at most 2 points are pushed onto the stack. Overall, at most 2 $ n $ points are pushed or popped. Thus, the time and space complexities of the algorithm are $O(n)$ $ even in case of degeneracies such as needles, where $ n $ is the number of the vertices of the polygon. Arrangement_2_ is the type used to represent the input environment. It must be an instance of CGAL::Arrangement_2 , where its CGAL::Arrangement_2::Traits_2 must be an instance of CGAL::Arr_segment_traits_2 , or of CGAL::Arr_non_caching_segment_traits_2 . RegularizationCategory indicates whether the output should be regularized. It can be specified by one of the following: #Tag_true or #Tag_false , where #Tag_false is the default value. Is model of Visibility_2 CGAL::Rotational_sweep_visibility_2 CGAL::Triangular_expansion_visibility_2

CGAL::Triangular_expansion_visibility_2.name: CGAL::Triangular_expansion_visibility_2
CGAL::Triangular_expansion_visibility_2.brief: This class is a model of the concept Visibility_2 can answer visibility queries within a polygon that may have holes.
CGAL::Triangular_expansion_visibility_2.detailed: The algorithm obtains a constrained triangulation from the input arrangement, then computes visibility by expanding the triangle that contains the query point. Preprocessing takes $O(n)$ time and $O(n)$ $ space, where $ n $ is the number of vertices of input polygon. The query time is $O(nh)$ , where $ h $ is the number of holes+1 of input polygon. Thus, for simple polygons (or a polygon with a constant number of holes) the algorithm complexity is linear, but it is $O(n^2)$ in the worst case, as the number of holes can be linear in $ n $ . Arrangement_2_ is the type used to represent the input environment. It must be an instance of CGAL::Arrangement_2 , where its CGAL::Arrangement_2::Traits_2 must be an instance of CGAL::Arr_segment_traits_2 , or of CGAL::Arr_non_caching_segment_traits_2 . RegularizationCategory indicates whether the output should be regularized. It can be specified by one of the following: #Tag_true or #Tag_false , where #Tag_false is the default value. Is model of Visibility_2 CGAL::Simple_polygon_visibility_2 CGAL::Rotational_sweep_visibility_2

members.brief: identifies whether the regularized visibility area is computed (either #Tag_true or #Tag_false ).

members.name: Supports_simple_polygon_category
members.brief: See Visibility_2::Supports_simple_polygon_category .

members.name: attach
members.brief: Attaches the given arrangement to the visibility object and computes the restricted triangulation.
members.detailed: This takes $O(n)$ $ time, where $ n $ is the number of vertices. From this moment on the class observes changes in the arrangement. If the arrangement changes a new restricted triangulation is computed. Re-attaching forces re-computation. In case the object is already attached to another arrangement, the visibility object gets detached before being attached to arr .

Visibility_2.name: Visibility_2
Visibility_2.brief: A model of the Visibility_2 concept can be attached to an Arrangement_2 instance to answer visibility queries within the faces of this arrangement.

members.name: Arrangement_2
members.brief: The arrangement type of the input.

members.name: Point_2
members.brief: The 2D point type used for the queries.

members.name: Face_const_handle
members.brief: The face handle type of the input arrangement.

members.name: Halfedge_const_handle
members.brief: The halfedge handle type of the input arrangement.

members.name: Regularization_category
members.brief: identifies whether the regularized visibility area is computed (either #Tag_true or #Tag_false ).

members.name: Supports_general_polygon_category
members.brief: identifies whether general polygons (with holes) are supported (either #Tag_true or #Tag_false ).
members.detailed: A general polygon is represented by a face $ f $ with no isolated vertex and any edge of $ f $ separates $ f $ from another face. Note that this allows $ f $ to touch a vertex from several sides.

members.name: Supports_simple_polygon_category
members.brief: identifies whether simple polygons are supported (either #Tag_true or #Tag_false ).
members.detailed: A simple polygon is represented by a face $ f $ with no holes, no isolated vertex and any edge of $ f $ separates $ f $ from another face. Note that this allows $ f $ to touch a vertex from several sides.

members.name: Visibility_2
members.brief: Default constructor creates an empty Visibility_2 object that is not attached to any arrangement yet.

members.name: Visibility_2
members.brief: Constructs a Visibility_2 object that is attached to arr .

members.name: is_attached
members.brief: Returns whether an arrangement is attached to the visibility object.

members.name: attach
members.brief: Attaches the given arrangement arr to the visibility object.
members.detailed: In case the object is already attached to another arrangement, the visibility object gets detached before being attached to arr .

members.name: detach
members.brief: Detaches the arrangement from the visibility object it is currently attached to.

members.name: arrangement_2
members.brief: Access to the attached arrangement.

members.name: compute_visibility
members.brief: Computes the visibility region for the given query point q in the face $ f $ of the arrangement that is attached to the visibility object.
members.detailed: The visibility region of q is stored in out_arr , that is, all features but the unbounded face of out_arr represent the visibility region. VisibilityArrangement_2 is the type of the output arrangement representing the visibility polygon. It must be an instance of CGAL::Arrangement_2 , where its CGAL::Arrangement_2::Traits_2 must be mutual convertible to VisibilityArrangement_2::Traits_2 . q is the query point f is the face of the arrangement in which the visibility region is computed out_arr is the output arrangement f is a face of this-> arrangement_2() q is in the interior of the given face f the face handle to the face in out_arr that represents interior of the visibility region
members.argsstring: (const Point_2 &q, const Face_const_handle f, VisibilityArrangement_2 &out_arr) const

members.name: compute_visibility
members.brief: Computes the visibility region in e->face() for the given query point q which must be located on e .
members.detailed: If q is an interior point of e , the computed visibility region is restricted to the side indicated by the halfedge e . If q is an endpoint of e , the visibility region is restricted by e and e->next() . The visibility region of q is stored in out_arr , that is, all features but the unbounded face of out_arr represent the visibility region. VisibilityArrangement_2 is the type of the output arrangement representing the visibility polygon. It must be an instance of CGAL::Arrangement_2 , where its CGAL::Arrangement_2::Traits_2 must be mutual convertible to VisibilityArrangement_2::Traits_2 . q is the query point e the halfedge on which q is located out_arr is the output arrangement e is a halfedge of this-> arrangement_2() q is on e q equals to e->target()->point() if q is an endpoint of e a handle to the face in out_arr that represents the interior of the visibility region
members.argsstring: (const Point_2 &q, const Halfedge_const_handle e, Visibility_arrangement_2 &out_arr) const
